<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- boundary-check.test.ts is a living enforcement document for the zone layering rules described in CLAUDE.md — run it on every PR that touches src/viewer/ or src/server/
- build-output-contract.test.ts guards against silent regressions in the Vite build (missing chunks, renamed entry points) — update it whenever the build configuration changes intentionally
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling confirms these tests are correctly isolated from production code and import only from test infrastructure
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling indicate this zone is correctly self-contained — integration tests import no production modules directly, relying instead on build artifacts and filesystem checks.
- boundary-check.test.ts directly enforces the messaging exemption rule documented in CLAUDE.md (lines 74–80); any new file added to src/viewer/messaging/ should be reviewed against this test to confirm it qualifies for the exemption.
- With only 2 files, this zone is lean; as the web package grows, consider adding an integration test that validates the MCP HTTP session lifecycle end-to-end within the package boundary.
